feed.title: Ivan R. Judson
feed.subtitle: Writing on AI systems, hardware, firmware, PCB design, and independent building. Based in Three Forks, Montana.

entry.title: Why Every WLED Controller Ships Blind (And What It Takes to Fix That)

link.title: Why Every WLED Controller Ships Blind (And What It Takes to Fix That)

entry.summary: Second in a series on building the Buffalo Jump Forge LED Controller from idea to Kickstarter. The market research told us current monitoring was the gap. What it didn’t tell us was why nobody had filled it. Once we started designing the board, that became clear pretty fast. The problem A WLED controller is basically an ESP32 with level shifters and MOSFETs. The ESP32 generates the data ...

entry.title: Finding the Gap
entry.summary: First in a series on building the Buffalo Jump Forge LED Controller from idea to Kickstarter. Ryan Pedersen and I go back to our Microsoft days. We’ve been tinkering with LED strips for years — longer than either of us would probably admit. The problem we kept running into was always the same: we wanted to run a lot of lights, outdoors, simply. Nothing on the market did that well. The contro...

entry.title: Terraprint: From Drone Flight to 3D-Printed Ranch in One Pipeline

link.title: Terraprint: From Drone Flight to 3D-Printed Ranch in One Pipeline

entry.summary: Terraprint: From Drone Flight to 3D-Printed Ranch in One Pipeline I run cattle on a piece of ground near Three Forks, Montana called Buffalo Jump Ranch. It is a rolling, awkward shape of land that does not survey well in your head. I wanted a physical model of it on my desk — something I could point at when a neighbor asked about a fence line, or when I was planning where to put a new tank. T...

entry.title: Astronomus: I Built a Robot to Run My Telescope So I Can Sleep

link.title: Astronomus: I Built a Robot to Run My Telescope So I Can Sleep

entry.summary: I’ve been doing astrophotography for a while now. It started as a hobby and quickly became an obsession, the way these things do. The problem is that astrophotography has a lot of friction — planning which targets are up tonight, figuring out when they’ll be optimally positioned, checking weather, watching satellite tracks, staying up until 2am to babysit the scope so it doesn’t point into a tr...

entry.title: Preparing for Opportunity: A GitHub Profile Toolkit

link.title: Preparing for Opportunity: A GitHub Profile Toolkit

entry.summary: “Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.” - Seneca I call it serendipity. You can’t control when the right opportunity appears. But you can control whether you’re prepared when it does. Repository: github-profile-upgrade The Preparation Gap Most engineers prepare for opportunity by building skills, shipping code, solving problems. Crucial. But incomplete. What they’re mi...
